telegram.text:
📝Referendum Against Keir Starmer📝
And he lost it

Local and regional elections took place in the United Kingdom. At stake were more than 5,000 seats in 136 councils, all London boroughs, six mayors in England plus the parliaments of Scotland and Wales.

🔻Voting results:
▪️The Reform Party achieved a breakthrough and won approximately 1,450 seats — a colossal gain compared to the previous cycle, when the faction had only a handful of deputies. Labour finished the elections with 1,068 seats, losing around 1,500 seats compared to the previous elections.

▪️Conservatives retained 801 seats but lost approximately 560 seats. Liberal Democrats won 834 seats and gained around 150 seats, Greens increased their representation by approximately 400 seats, and independents and local groups took around 212 seats, increasing their share by roughly thirty seats.

▪️Separately on the same day, parliamentary elections took place in Scotland. Following the vote, the Scottish National Party remains the largest force but without an absolute majority, while parties advocating a tougher agenda on autonomy and socio-economic policy are gaining strength.

▪️Similar elections were held for the Welsh parliament, where a regional legislative assembly is also being formed for a new term. The results there are less dramatic than in England and Scotland: local autonomist parties and the Labour camp as a whole maintain their positions, but the overall trend is the same — declining trust in traditional parties and growing support for more radical and protest movements.

In terms of a hypothetical national result, the Reform Party takes first place — approximately 27% of votes. Conservatives receive approximately 20%, Labour — 15–16%, Liberal Democrats and Greens — roughly 14% each.

📌The result for the first time elevates the Reform Party to the status of the largest force at the local level and simultaneously records Labour's largest decline in seats among all major parties.

Prime Minister Keir Starmer acknowledged the results as "tough", took responsibility for the defeat, but refused to resign, saying he will not "plunge the country into chaos" and such days only "strengthen his resolve".

❗️For British politics, this is a "Farage moment". His party is picking up protest voters both from disappointed Labour working-class areas and from traditionally conservative periphery.

If the trend consolidates, traditional parties will face an even harder time in the next general elections, although a decisive success for the Reform Party is unlikely given the peculiarities of the British electoral system, which limits the direct influence of minor parties.

@rybar

related_markets.question: No-Confidence Vote against Starmer by June 30?
related_markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if a motion of no-confidence against the sitting government is voted upon in the House of Commons of the United Kingdom by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve based on whether a motion of no confidence is voted upon in the specified timeframe. Whether the motion is passed will not affect this market’s resolution.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from the government of the United Kingdom and a consensus of credible reporting.
related_markets.impact_reason: Labour's significant seat loss indicates potential for no-confidence vote (1 hop).
related_markets.event_title: No-Confidence Vote against Starmer by June 30?

related_markets.title: May 15
related_markets.question: Starmer out by May 15, 2026?
related_markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Keir Starmer ceases to be the Prime Minister of the United Kingdom for any period of time between market creation and May 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Keir Starmer's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be the government of the UK, however a consensus of credible reporting will also suffice.
related_markets.impact_reason: Labour's decline in seats suggests increased risk of Starmer's removal (1 hop).

tweet.body: Tyson Sinks, Walmart Falls After Trump Moves To Temporarily Lower Beef Import Tariffs https://www.zerohedge.com/commodities/tyson-sinks-walmart-falls-after-trump-moves-temporarily-lower-beef-import-tariffs

related_markets.title: 1.1T+
related_markets.question: Will the 2026 trade deficit be 1.1T or more?
related_markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the annual US Goods and Services Deficit for 2026, as reported by the US Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA) and the US Census Bureau (USCB) in the “U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services” release for December and Annual 2026, expected to be released in February 2027.

Upon publication, the specified release will be made available at: https://www.bea.gov/news/current-releases

The relevant figure may be found in the annual summary under “Exports, Imports, and Balance (exhibit 1)”. Changes in the BEA or USCB’s reporting format will not disqualify a relevant published figure from counting.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the “U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services” release for December and Annual 2026 from the US Bureau of Economic Analysis and the US Census Bureau. If this release is not published by April 30, 2027 ET, another credible source on the annual US Goods and Services Deficit for 2026 will be chosen.

Note: any revisions to the annual US Goods and Services Deficit for 2026 made after the publication of the “U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services” release for December and Annual 2026 will not be considered.
related_markets.impact_reason: Tariff changes directly influence trade balance (1 hop).

tweet.body: AUSTRALIA WARNS IRAN CONFLICT COULD SEND OIL TO $200 Australia’s government warned that a worsening Iran conflict could drive oil prices to $200 a barrel and trigger a global economic shock. In its annual budget, the Treasury said prolonged fighting or damage to Middle East energy routes could disrupt oil supplies, push inflation above 7% and cause Australia’s economy to shrink. Oil prices already climbed after President Trump rejected Iran’s latest proposal and doubts grew over the ceasefire. Shipping through the Strait of Hormuz also remained disrupted. Treasurer Jim Chalmers said Australia’s economic outlook now depends heavily on how the Middle East conflict develops.

related_markets.title: September 30
related_markets.question: Will Crude Oil reach a new all-time high by September 30?
related_markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day after market creation, the official daily high price published by the CME Group for the Active Month (front month) of CME Crude Oil (CL) futures is greater than $147.27 by the final trading day on or before the specified date. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For CME Crude Oil (CL) futures contracts, the active month is the nearest of the contract months listed. The active month becomes a non-active month effective two business days prior to the spot month expiration. For example, if the spot month expires on a Friday the next listed contract will be considered the Active Month on the Wednesday prior to the spot month expiration.

This market will resolve as soon as a high price greater than the listed value is published, or once finalized data for the final trading day of the specified time period is published and a high price greater than $147.27 has not been achieved.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website (https://www.cmegroup.com/markets/energy/crude-oil/light-sweet-crude.quotes.html) — specifically, the daily "High" prices for the Active Month of Crude Oil (CL) futures.
related_markets.impact_reason: Worsening conflict in Iran directly impacts oil prices, 1 hop.
related_markets.event_title: Crude Oil all time high by...?

related_markets.question: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by May 15?
related_markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Strait of Hormuz equal to or above 60 for any date between market creation and May 15, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Daily transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls equal to or above the specified level, or once data has been published for the final date in the specified period and no such value has been published. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. However, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for May 15, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.
related_markets.impact_reason: Disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz directly impact oil supply, 1 hop.
related_markets.event_title: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by May 15?
